article_title: Inside the Race to Build Europe's First Gigafactory for Solid-State Batteries

checklist_items.task: All factual claims verified against primary sources
checklist_items.notes: 32 claims checked; 2 minor corrections applied (energy density figure updated to 410 Wh/kg per manufacturer spec sheet; EU subsidy amount corrected from €1.8B to €1.6B)

checklist_items.task: All named sources confirmed quotes and granted publication consent
checklist_items.notes: 6 named sources re-confirmed via email; CEO Katrin Brandt requested removal of one off-the-record aside — removed from final draft

checklist_items.task: Photography rights cleared for all images
checklist_items.notes: 12 images licensed from Reuters and 4 from company press kit; all usage rights documented in asset manifest ref TECH-2026-0310-IMG
3 charts verified: EU battery capacity timeline, cost-per-kWh comparison, and supply chain map. Source data attached in supplementary files

checklist_items.task: Headline and subheadline reviewed for accuracy and tone
checklist_items.notes: Original headline revised from 'Europe Bets Big on Solid-State' to current version for specificity
Author holds no financial interest in any company mentioned. Disclosure statement appended to article footer

checklist_items.task: SEO metadata and social sharing text prepared
checklist_items.notes: Meta description, Open Graph tags, and three social post variants submitted to CMS

checklist_items.task: Accessibility check — alt text for all images and chart descriptions
